coredata.dc:title: Hydrogel-involved portable ratiometric fluorescence sensor based on Eu3+/CDs-modified metal-organic framework for detection of ofloxacin and tetracycline
coredata.prism:copyright: © 2024 Elsevier B.V. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.
coredata.prism:publisher: Elsevier B.V. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.
coredata.dc:creator: Liu, Junxue
Li, Wen
Li, Jianyun
Li, Jiyang
coredata.dc:description: Highly selective and sensitive monitoring ofloxacin and tetracycline residues is meaningful for food safety and environmental protection. Herein, benefitting from strong coordination capability and good porosity of metal-organic framework UiO-67b, the dual-functional fluoroprobes were developed based on UiO-67b post-modified by luminescent Eu3+ and carbon dots (CDs). Eu3+/CDs-modified UiO-67b exhibited dual recognition sites with controllable red and blue emissions, which could be quenched by ofloxacin and tetracycline through internal filtering effect resulting in distinct color change from red to blue. Based on this, the ratiometric fluorescent assay was first established towards ofloxacin and tetracycline in the ranges of 0–60/0–10 µM, reaching a low limit of detection of 22/27 nM. Satisfactory recoveries of 98.5–103.7 % in real samples as well as visual analysis endow such intelligent sensing platform great potential on practical evaluating antibiotic residues in food.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Antibiotics
Metal-organic framework
Ratiometric fluorescence
Visual analysis
Smartphone

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Introduction

Syntheses of UiO-67b, Eu/UiO-67b and CDs@Eu/UiO-67b
Fluorescent detection of OFL and TC with Eu/UiO-67b-3
Fluorescent detection of OFL and TC with CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b
Hydrogel-involved smartphone-assisted platform for visual OFL detection
OFL and TC determination in real samples
Results and discussion
Characterizations of materials
Fluorescence detection of OFL and TC by Eu/UiO-67b-3
Ratiometric fluorescence detection of OFL and TC by CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b
Possible mechanism of sensing OFL and TC
Hydrogel-involved smartphone-assisted platform for visual OFL detection
Determination of OFL and TC on real samples
Conclusions
CRediT authorship contribution statement

Supplementary material

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: XRD patterns (A, B) and fluorescence emission spectra under 330 nm excitation (H, I) of Eu/UiO-67b and CDs@Eu/UiO-67b, respectively (Insert: photos of CDs@Eu/UiO-67b with different amount of CDs). (C) Zeta potential of CDs and prepared samples. (D) EDS of CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b. XPS survey spectra (E), XPS spectra of N 1 s (F) and Zr 3d (G) of UiO-67b, Eu/UiO-67b-3 and CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b. (J) Excitation-emission matrix map of CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b.

Fluorescence spectra under 330 nm excitation of Eu/UiO-67b-3 with different concentration of (A) OFL and (D) TC. (B) F493/F612 versus OFL concentrations and (E) F/F0 versus TC concentrations. Inset: photographs of Eu/UiO-67b-3 suspension under 254 nm before/after adding OFL/TC. CIE of Eu/UiO-67b-3 with different concentrations of (C) OFL and (F) TC. (G) Selectivity, (H) Anti-interference and (I) recovery performance of Eu/UiO-67b-3 sensing OFL.

Fluorescence spectra under 330 nm excitation of CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b with different concentration of (A) OFL and (D) TC. (B) F489/F612 versus OFL concentrations and (E) F/F0 versus TC concentrations. Inset: photographs of CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b suspension under 254 nm before/after adding OFL and TC. CIE of CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b with different concentrations of (C) OFL and (F) TC. Selectivity and anti-interference of (G) OFL and (H) TC detection. (I) Recovery performance of CDs-1@Eu/UIO-67b sensing TC.

XRD patterns of Eu/UiO-67b-3 (A) and CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b (D) before and after OFL/TC treatment. Absorbance spectra of OFL/TC and the optimal excitation/emission spectra of (B) Eu/UiO-67b-3 and (E) CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b. Fluorescence lifetimes of (C) Eu/UiO-67b-3 and (F) CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b with and without OFL/TC addition. (G) Fluorescence sensing sketch of Eu/UiO-67b-3 and CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b for OFL and TC.

Diagrammatic sketch of visually detecting OFL assisted by smartphone of Eu/UiO-67b-3 based (A) aqueous solution and (B) hydrogel. Inset are the corresponding photos. Fitting curve between OFL concentration and color change (R + G)/2B of (C) solution and (D) hydrogel.
Illustration of preparation of Eu/UiO-67b and CDs@Eu/UiO-67b, sensing of OFL and TC, and visual application assisted by a smartphone.

head.ce:title: Hydrogel-involved portable ratiometric fluorescence sensor based on Eu3+/CDs-modified metal-organic framework for detection of ofloxacin and tetracycline

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: State Key Laboratory of Inorganic Synthesis and Preparative Chemistry, College of Chemistry, Jilin University, 2699 Qianjin Street, Changchun 130012, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: State Key Laboratory of Inorganic Synthesis and Preparative Chemistry, College of Chemistry, Jilin University

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: State Key Laboratory of Inorganic Synthesis and Preparative Chemistry, College of Chemistry, Jilin University, 2699 Qianjin Street, Changchun 130012, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Highly selective and sensitive monitoring ofloxacin and tetracycline residues is meaningful for food safety and environmental protection. Herein, benefitting from strong coordination capability and good porosity of metal-organic framework UiO-67b, the dual-functional fluoroprobes were developed based on UiO-67b post-modified by luminescent Eu3+ and carbon dots (CDs). Eu3+/CDs-modified UiO-67b exhibited dual recognition sites with controllable red and blue emissions, which could be quenched by ofloxacin and tetracycline through internal filtering effect resulting in distinct color change from red to blue. Based on this, the ratiometric fluorescent assay was first established towards ofloxacin and tetracycline in the ranges of 0–60/0–10 µM, reaching a low limit of detection of 22/27 nM. Satisfactory recoveries of 98.5–103.7 % in real samples as well as visual analysis endow such intelligent sensing platform great potential on practical evaluating antibiotic residues in food.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The ratiometric fluorescent assay was first established for detection both of low-concentration OFL and TC by introducing luminescent Eu3+ and carbon dots (CDs) into UiO-67b. The practicability in water and food samples, and portability based on a smartphone of such probe has been investigated with reliable results.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Antibiotics as a main type of pollutants have heavily harmed the security of food and water due to their abuse [1,2]. Ofloxacin (OFL) and tetracycline (TC) are common antibiotics, which have been frequently utilized in animal husbandry and humans [3], however, the overuse and hard metabolism of them results in extensive residue in drinking water, animal-derived foods, and human body, which poses a substantial threat to human health and natural environment [4]. The maximum residue for OFL and TC in food implemented by the EU is as low as the µg/kg level, and the Chinese Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Affairs has banned the use OFL in food animals [5]. Therefore, it is of great importance to monitor low concentrations of OFL and TC in food and drinking water.

Fluorescent assays have generated great attention in antibiotic analysis owing to their low equipment cost, simple procedures, and time-saving [6,7]. Among various fluorescent sensors, metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) have become a promising candidate due to their good stability, easy post-modification, and structure tunability [8–10]. By now, MOF-based fluoroprobes have been developed to detect TC and realized nanomolar limits of detection [11], while there are rare reports for OFL detection [12]. Furthermore, most of MOF-based sensors show single emission that can be affected by uncontrolled variables [13,14]. Comparatively, ratiometric fluoroprobes greatly increase the accuracy and repeatability of analytical procedures by collecting fluorescence from two or more channels and offering intrinsic self-calibration of the signal output [15,16]. However, it is still a challenge for developing a dual-functional MOF-based sensor to realize the ratiometric fluorescence detection for both of OFL and TC.

Given the structure designability of MOFs, a powerful strategy for constructing ratiometric fluorescence sensing platform is to incorporate muti-recognition units into a MOF material [17–19]. Lanthanide ions (Ln3+) with pure color and unique emission spectra and carbon dots (CDs) with low toxicity and excellent luminescence properties are common luminescent centers, which have been used for various sensing applications [20]. Among various MOFs, UiO-67b (b = 2,2-bipyridine-4,4′-dicarboxylic acid) shows intrinsic water and structure stability [21,22]. Meanwhile, the distinctive ligands with strong capability of coordination and good porosity endow UiO-67b excellent potential for easily post-modified with Ln3+ and CDs to construct a deal fluoroprobe.

Herein, the aim of this work is to develop novel and powerful ratiometrically fluorescent platform for detection of OFL and TC by smart integrating multi-luminescent probes of Eu3+ and CDs into UiO-67b ( Scheme 1). The fluoroprobes were prepared by a facile and simple step-by-step post-modification. First, red-emitting Eu3+ were introduced and coordinated with the ligands, which could be quenched by OFL with blue emission to achieve a ratiometric fluorescent detection. Moreover, blue-emitting CDs was further doped in Eu/UiO-67b to form dual-peak emissions. CDs@Eu/UiO-67b firstly realized the ratiometric fluorescent detection for both of OFL and TC, and reached lower LOD of 22 nM and 27 nM, respectively. Moreover, a portable hydrogel-involved device for visual assessment of OFL was developed.
All reagents and apparatus are described in detail in the Supporting Information.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Syntheses of UiO-67b, Eu/UiO-67b and CDs@Eu/UiO-67b
ce:section.ce:para: UiO-67b was prepared according to the previous report [23]. 0.097 g ZrOCl2·8 H2O, 0.073 g H2bpydc and 0.5 mL HAc were dissolved in 10 mL DMF, stirred for 20 min and transferred into a Teflon-lined stainless vessel before heating at 120 °C for 24 h. Eu/UiO-67b was prepared through heating the DMF solution (10 mL) containing UiO-67b (50 mg) and EuCl3·6 H2O (20, 40, 60, 80, 100 mg) at 120 °C for 24 h. The solid products were named Eu/UiO-67b-1 to Eu/UiO-67b-5. CDs was obtained according to our previous report [24].

CDs@Eu/UiO-67b was prepared by mixing the DMF solution (10 mL) containing Eu/UiO-67b-3 (30 mg) and CDs (1, 2, 3 mg) at room temperature for 10 h. The solid products were named CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b to CDs-3@Eu/UiO-67b. All the solid products were obtained by centrifuging under 8000 rpm for 4 min and washing three times with DMF and ethanol, and dried in vacuum.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescent detection of OFL and TC with Eu/UiO-67b-3
ce:section.ce:para: Different concentrations of OFL and TC were added to 150 µg/mL Eu/UiO-67b-3 solution to achieve the total volume of 2 mL. The fluorescent emission spectra of the mixture were recorded after incubating at room temperature for 4 min (λex = 320 nm). The relationship between the fluorescent intensity ratio (F493/F612) and (F/F0) with the concentration of OFL and TC was investigated systematically.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescent detection of OFL and TC with CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b
ce:section.ce:para: Different concentrations of OFL and TC were added to 150 µg/mL CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b solution to achieve the total volume of 2 mL. The fluorescent emission spectra of the mixture were monitored same as before. The relationship between the fluorescent intensity ratio (F489/F612) and (F612/F442) with the concentration of OFL and TC was obtained, respectively.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Hydrogel-involved smartphone-assisted platform for visual OFL detection
ce:section.ce:para: 50 µL of sodium alginate aqueous solution (2 %, w/v), 10 µL of Eu/UiO-67b-3 (2 mg/mL), 20 µL of CaCl2 (50 mM) were added in the snap cap of centrifuge tube, and mixed evenly to form hydrogels [25]. Then, the centrifuge tubes were inverted at room temperature after the addition of different concentrations of OFL. The color information was achieved and the red, green, and blue (RGB) values were measured by a Color Recognition APP in a smartphone to realize the quantitative evaluation of OFL.

ce:section.ce:section-title: OFL and TC determination in real samples
ce:section.ce:para: To investigate the feasibility of our method in real samples, tap water taken from our laboratory and chicken samples purchased from the school supermarket were chosen for recovery experiments. These actual samples were first pretreated [11], then different concentrations of analytes standard solution were added to these real samples. The spiked samples were determined as described in Sections 2.4 and 2.5.

Two series of materials, Eu/UiO-67b and CDs@Eu/UiO-67b, with different Eu3+ and CDs contents were prepared. The crystal structures of materials were analyzed by XRD patterns ( Fig. 1A, B). The peak position and intensity had a good match with that of simulated UiO-67, indicating that the introduction of Eu3+ and CDs had no effect on the lattice structure of UiO-67b [26]. ICP analysis confirmed the existence of Eu in Eu/UiO-67b samples, and the highest content of Eu3+ was 5.35 wt% found in Eu/UiO-67b-3 (Table S1). The prepared CDs were brownish-black in color, which had uniform size about 2 nm, and a broad diffraction peak was observed, corresponding to the (002) planes of graphite lattice spacing (Fig. S1). Fig. 1B shows the color of CDs@Eu/UiO-67b powder gradually changed from white to yellow as doped CDs increasing, suggesting the successful incorporation of CDs [27].

In the following characterizations, Eu/UiO-67b-3 and CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b employed as a representative for research because the highest Eu3+ loading and good luminescent performance. According to CHN analyses (Table S2), the concentration of CDs in CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b was based on the extra content of C and N (C: 0.9 %; N: 0.03 %). As illustrated in Fig. S2, Eu/UiO-67b-3 and CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b showed the similar shape of ellipsoidal as that of UiO-67b, and their particle sizes were about 20 nm. The Zeta potential of UiO-67b is +2.98 mV, which changed to +18.60 mV after doping Eu3+, and then changed to +16.80 mV after incorporation of CDs with negatively charged (Fig. 1C). The energy spectroscopy (EDS) analysis proved elements of Zn, Eu, C, N and O exiting in CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b (Fig. 1D).

FT-IR spectra were used to determine the functional groups of materials. The prepared CDs contained the nitrogen-oxygen functional groups [28]. After the incorporation of CDs, the C-H/N-H vibration peaks of CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b at 3400 cm−1 became richer than those of UiO-67b and Eu/UiO-67b-3 (Fig. S3A). To further confirm the chemical composition of these samples, XPS analysis was conducted. As shown in Fig. 1E, all of them displayed the existence of C, N, O, and Zr, and peak of Eu 3d at 1136.5 eV was found in Eu/UiO-67b-3 and CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b [11]. Furthermore, the N 1 s in UiO-67b split from single peak of N-C into two peaks of N-C and N-Eu in Eu/UiO-67b-3 and CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b (Fig. 1F). Meanwhile, the Zr 3d in three materials exhibited the same position of peaks (Fig. 1G). These findings revealed that Eu3+ ions should be in the pores of UiO-67b and coordinated to the N in H2bpydc ligands [29]. In CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b, CDs were distributed in holes or loaded onto UiO-67b due to its high porosity and large specific surface area [30]. Because CDs were rich in the elements of C, N, and O (Fig. S3B), some nitrogen-containing functional groups of CDs were also coordinated with Eu3+, thereby CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b showed more N-Eu species than that of Eu/UiO-67b-3 (Fig. 1F).

The optical properties of Eu/UiO-67b-3 and CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b were investigated. As depicted in Fig. S4A, UiO-67b exhibited a weak emission at 450 nm originated from H2bpydc ligands. After the chelation of Eu3+, the typical sharp emission peaks of Eu3+ appeared at 591, 612, and 693 nm [13]. The intensity of peaks was correlated with Eu3+-content and Eu/UiO-67b-3 displayed the strongest luminescence (Fig. 1H). After doping CDs, CDs@Eu/UiO-67b showed two optical centers of CDs (peak at 442 nm) and Eu3+ ions (peak at 612 nm) (Fig. 1I). With the amount increasing of CDs, the peak intensity of 442 nm increased but that of 612 nm decreased gradually. Eu/UiO-67b-3 and CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b exhibited the excitation-independent feature with the optimal emission and excitation wavelength of 612 nm and 320 nm, respectively (Fig. 1J, S4B, C).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescence detection of OFL and TC by Eu/UiO-67b-3
ce:section.ce:para: It is interesting to found that OFL and TC could effectively quench the red fluorescence of Eu/UiO-67b-3, thereby a simple fluorescence assay was established ( Fig. 2A). The concentration of probe was determined as 150 µg/mL because the fluorescence intensity at 612 nm and the change of fluorescence after adding TC were suitable (Fig. S5A). With the concentration increasing of OFL, the fluorescence intensities of Eu/UiO-67b-3 decreased at 612 nm gradually, while the fluorescence intensities of OFL at 493 nm increased. As shown in Fig. 2B, the intensity ratio (F493/F612) of Eu/UiO-67b-3 exhibited a good linear relationship with the OFL concentration within the range from 0 to 60 μM. The limit of detection (LOD) is 40 nM. As listed in Table S2, MOFs-based fluorescent biosensors for OFL detection are very rare and most of them solely rely the changes of single signal. Compared with them, Eu/UiO-67b-3 probe not only realized the ratiometric fluorescence detection, but also exhibited wide range and comparable sensitivity towards OFL. Due to OFL emitting blue/green fluorescence under 254/365 nm irradiation (Fig. S5B, C), thus the color change of the detection system was obvious and abundant. With the increase of OFL, it changed from red to blue (Fig. 2B, C) and colorless to green (Fig. S6) under 254/365 nm irradiation. The excellent sensitivity and obvious color changes endowed Eu/UiO-67b-3 strong promise as a suitable material for fluorescence sensing applications.

Besides, the selectivity and anti-interference ability was studied by adding other interferences into the system without and with OFL, including Ca2+, Mg2+, Zn2+, K+, Na+, glucose, urea, alanine (Ala), aspartic acid (Asp), L-cysteine (Cys), tryptophan (Trp), kanamycin (Kana), sulfaquinoxaline (SQX), ciprofloxacin (CIP), norfloxacin (NOR), nitrofurazone (NIT), tetracycline (TC), sulfadiazine (SD), roxithromycin (ROX) and thiamphenicol (THI). Only the OFL group displayed significant change, illustrating the excellent selectivity of Eu/UiO-67b-3 (Fig. 2G). Fig. 2H reflected the good anti-interference ability based on the similar intensity of all groups. Notably, the assay showed a quick response time of 200 s (Fig. S7A). Compared with the original Eu/UiO-67b-3, the fluorescence intensity decreased to 90 % after recovery of five times. Meanwhile, the intensity ratio of Eu/UiO-67b-3 sensing OFL changed from 1.00 to 0.91, decreasing only 9 % (Fig. 2I). These results proved that Eu/UiO-67b-3 fluoroprobe possessed the advantages of fast-responding and good recyclability.

For TC detection, the system of Eu/UiO-67b-3 had only a single optical center due to TC with non-luminous. With the increasing of TC, the fluorescence intensity of Eu/UiO-67b-3 decreased gradually (Fig. 2D). The intensity ratio F/F0 (F and F0 represented the fluorescence intensity at 612 nm before and after TC addition) exhibited a good linear relationship with TC concentration, giving a LOD of 80 nM in the range from 0 to 10 μM (Fig. 2E). The color of the system changed from deep red to light red (Fig. 2B, F). This assay also displayed fast-responding and good selectivity (Figs. S7b and S8A). However, different from OFL detection, the sensing system showed relatively low sensitivity and only relied on the quenching of fluorescence like most previous reports [31].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Ratiometric fluorescence detection of OFL and TC by CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b
ce:section.ce:para: To realize the ratiometric fluorescence detection for both of OFL and TC, a series of CDs@Eu/UiO-67b were prepared by further introducing of blue-emission CDs into Eu/UiO-67b-3. For OFL detection, the operation of the sensing platform was mainly based on the fluorescence changes of OFL and Eu3+ in CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b because the emission peak of OFL is close to that of CDs but with stronger intensity ( Fig. 3A). 150 µg/mL CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b was determined as suitable probe because the change of F612/F442 was the largest (Fig. S8B, C). The intensity ratio (F489/F612) of CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b exhibited a good linear relationship with the OFL concentration within the range from 0 to 60 µΜ with LOD of 22 nM and the color of the detection system changed from red to blue (Fig. 3B, C). For TC detection, the situation was different from that of Eu/UiO-67b-3. The red and blue emissions of CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b was significantly and slightly quenched by TC, respectively (Fig. 3D). The F612/F442 showed a good linear relationship with TC concentration in the range of 0–10 μM with LOD of 27 nM and the color of system faded gradually (Fig. 3E, F). Notably, the above values of LOD for OFL and TC are obvious lower than that of Eu/UiO-67b-3, and comparable with the best report summarized in Table S3 and Table S4. Similar with Eu/UiO-67b-3, CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b also displayed good selectivity, anti-interference ability and excellent recycling performance (Fig. 3G-I).

We also studied the sensing ability of CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b for other fluoroquinolone and tetracycline category antibiotics with structures akin to ofloxacin and tetracycline (Fig. S9A). The results showed that NOR and CIP could not quench the red fluorescence at 612 nm (Fig. S9B). OTC and CTC exhibited the similar fluorescence quenching as TC, but their effects were weaker (Fig. S9C). The higher selectivity for TC was attributed to the larger overlapping between the absorption of TC and the excitation spectra of probes (Fig. S9D, E). Moreover, the fluorescence intensity of three batches of probes was not much difference, showing the good reproducibility of material preparation (Fig. S10).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Possible mechanism of sensing OFL and TC
ce:section.ce:para: The possible fluorescence response mechanisms of Eu/UiO-67b-3 and CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b to OFL and TC were investigated. XRD patterns of two samples were identical before and after OFL and TC treatment, demonstrating their good stability and the change of fluorescence intensity was not originated from the structure collapse of MOFs ( Fig. 4A, D). In addition, the broad-spectrum absorption bands of OFL and TC significantly overlapped with the excitation spectra of Eu/UiO-67b-3 and CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b, suggesting that they could absorb a considerable amount of excitation energy of probes, thereby quenching the fluorescence intensity (Fig. 4B, E). Moreover, the fluorescence lifetimes of Eu/UiO-67b-3 and CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b showed no significant change after OFL and TC addition (Fig. 4C, F), and the absence of new absorption peak suggested that no new complex formed during the quenching process, which excluded the possibility of static quenching (Fig. S11). These findings confirmed that the inner filtering effect (IFE) was the source of the quenching phenomena. Since the O and N atoms in OFL/TC could form strong metal complexes with Eu3+, thus an interaction was achieved between Eu/UiO-67b-3/CDs-1@Eu/UiO-67b and OFL/TC, which was demonstrated as the absorption peak increasing at the broad absorption band from 226 to 400 nm (Fig. S12) [5]. Based on these studies, the overall mechanistic process was presented in Fig. 4G.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Hydrogel-involved smartphone-assisted platform for visual OFL detection
ce:section.ce:para: To enhance the practicality and convenience of such sensing platform, we realized the visual analysis by employing the smartphone as a signal reader and analyzer. Owing to more noticeable color changes of the fluorescence of Eu/UiO-67b-3 after adding OFL, this system was selected for the visual analysis. Fig. 5A showed that the fluorescent color of Eu/UiO-67b-3 aqueous solution changed from red to light green with the addition of OFL and the color change (R + G)/2B was linearly related to the OFL concentration from 0 to 60 μM with a LOD of 0.31 μM (Fig. 5C).

Considering the good selectivity, sensitivity, and apparent color evolution, we further developed a portable and user-friendly analytical device for OFL visual detection. Hydrogel with porous structure and transparency was selected as the media to assemble Eu/UiO-67b-3 because it could avoid the aggregation of probes and facilitate the visual inspection of colors, also could offer color and distance change as signals for sensing application [32–34]. Eu/UiO-67b-3 based hydrogel was prepared, then it was immersed by different concentrations of OFL and reacting (Fig. 5B). With the increasing of OFL concentration, the color of hydrogel exhibited a significant evolution from red to blue. The color information was recognized by a smartphone and the color change (R + G)/2B was linearly related to OFL concentration of 0–60 μM with a LOD of 0.76 μM (Fig. 5D). These results indicated that Eu/UiO-67b-3 probe showed comparable analytical performance and good potential to conduct an accurate, portable, and on-site analysis of OFL in the field of food safety.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Determination of OFL and TC on real samples
ce:section.ce:para: To further validate the utility of the sensing system for the detection of OFL and TC, standardized spiked tests were performed. Tap water and chicken were employed as real samples and the sensing process was described in section 2.7 (The details for sample preparation were provided in supporting information). As shown in Table S4 and Table S5, the sensing platforms displayed favorable recovery of 99.80–103.66 % for OFL and 98.50–100.29 % for TC with low relative standard deviation (RSD) less than 5 %. All above results demonstrated the assay meet the demands of practical applications.

In summary, towards the monitoring antibiotic residues of OFL and TC for food safety, two powerful ratiometric fluorescence probes were constructed by integration of red-emitting Eu3+ and blue-emitting CDs into UiO-67b via simple post-modification. The sensing platform was operated owing to the influences of OFL/TC on both of red and blue fluorescence via IFE mechanism. Combined with inherit blue luminescence of OFL, a simple ratiometric fluorescent detection of OFL was achieved by Eu/UiO-67b with a low LOD of 40 nM. Moreover, a dual-functional ratiometric fluorescent sensor was firstly realized by CDs@Eu/UiO-67b for detection of low-concentration OFL and TC, which exhibited enhanced sensitivity and accuracy with lower LOD of 22 and 27 nM, respectively. These proposed fluorescence probes showed highly sensitivity, selectivity, anti-interference ability and excellent recycling performance for the detection of OFL/TC. Hydrogel-involved smartphone-assisted visual analysis was further developed for OFL determination and the detection of OFL/TC in environment and food samples was conducted with satisfactory results revealing good applicability. This work would greatly promote more MOFs-based ratiometric fluoroprobes and portable devices to visually and quantitatively monitor antibiotics in food and water.

Jiyang Li: Validation, Supervision, Funding acquisition. Wen Li: Formal analysis, Data curation. Jianyun Li: Formal analysis. Junxue Liu: Writing – original draft, Validation, Investigation, Formal analysis, Conceptualization.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare no competing financial interest.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: We thank the 111 Project (No. B17020) and Jilin Province Science and Technology Development Plan (20230101026JC) for financial support.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data associated with this article can be found in the online version at doi:10.1016/j.snb.2024.136573.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Demand, status, and prospect of antibiotics detection in the environment
Sens. Actuators B Chem.

ACS Sens

ce:biography.ce:simple-para: Junxue Liu is currently carrying out her Doctor's degree under the guidance of Professor Jiyang Li in Jilin University (China). Her research is mainly focused on the synthesis of CDs and CDs@MOF nanocomposites and their application in the field of nanozymes.
Wen Li obtained his Doctor's degree in chemistry from Jilin University in 2024. His research is mainly focused on the synthesis and application of metal-organic frameworks (MOFs).

Jianyun Li is currently carrying out her Doctor's degree under the guidance of Professor Yunling Liu in Jilin University (China). Her research is mainly focused on the synthesis and application of MOFs.

Jiyang Li is a professor at State Key Laboratory of Inorganic Synthesis and Preparative Chemistry, Jilin University. She received her PhD degree from Jilin University (China) in 1999. Her research focuses on the synthesis and new properties of porous functional materials.
